windows.old_description: Melting/colouring wax for the whipped candle.
windows.embed_text_v2: Stirring melted soy wax in a stainless pouring pitcher with a teal silicone spatula, then hot-gluing a tabbed cotton wick to the bottom centre of an empty clear glass tumbler.
windows.summary_v2: Maker stirs melted soy wax in a pouring pitcher then switches to hot-gluing a wick tab to the bottom of a clear glass tumbler.

tags_v2.action: stirring melted wax then gluing wick

seconds_v2.desc: Top-down on a tiled backsplash counter: a stainless pouring pitcher of melted golden-yellow wax; hand brings in a teal silicone spatula and dips it in.
Hand stirring the melted wax with the teal spatula inside the metal pouring pitcher; smooth golden surface.
Continued slow stirring; spatula visible as a teal blade in the golden wax.
Spatula lifted partway out, hand rotating; lighter swirl visible in the wax — possibly cooler wax mixing with hotter wax.
Hand makes a broader stir, dragging a wave of pale almost-set wax across the surface — looks like whipped or thickening soy.
Cut to a side view: bare hand holds a white hot-glue gun aimed at the bottom of a wick tab; a clear glass tumbler sits above on the tile background.
Hand has just glued the wick tab — squeezes the trigger; clear glass tumbler waits behind, ready to receive the wick.
Hand holds the glued wick with tab between fingers near the empty clear glass tumbler — about to place it in.
Top-down: hand pressing the glued wick tab to the bottom centre of the clear glass tumbler to set the wick upright.
